Class DoublyLinkedList<T>

java.lang.Object
com.sebastiandorata.musicdashboard.utils.DoublyLinkedList<T>
public class DoublyLinkedList<T> extends Object
Generic DoublyLinkedList data structure for pagination. Purpose: Enables efficient forward/backward traversal through large result sets, supporting use cases like "view first/last N items" or "go to previous/next page". Time Complexity: - Access by index: O(n) in worst case (traversal required) - Insert at head/tail: O(1) - Insert at position: O(n) - Delete at position: O(n) - Next/previous page: O(1) amortized Space Complexity: O(n) where n = number of elements Use case: Analytics listings (songs, artists, albums, history) where users paginate through results and may traverse backward.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    add(T value)
    Appends value to the tail of the list.
    void
    addFirst(T value)
    Prepends value to the head of the list.
    boolean
    contains(T value)
    Returns true if the list contains an element equal to value according to Object.equals(Object).
    T
    get(int index)
    Returns the element at the given index using bidirectional traversal.
    T
    getFirst()
    Returns the first element in the list without removing it.
    T
    getLast()
    Returns the last element in the list without removing it.
    List<T>
    getWindow(int offset, int limit)
    Returns a window (page) of elements from the list.
    boolean
    isEmpty()
    Returns true if the list contains no elements.
    T
    removeFirst()
    Removes and returns the first element in the list.
    T
    removeLast()
    Removes and returns the last element in the list.
    List<T>
    toList()
    Returns all elements as a new List in head-to-tail order.

  • Constructor Details

    • DoublyLinkedList

      public DoublyLinkedList()

  • Method Details

    • add

      public void add(T value)
      Appends value to the tail of the list.

      Time Complexity: O(1) Space Complexity: O(1)

      Parameters:
      value - the value to append

    • addFirst

      public void addFirst(T value)
      Prepends value to the head of the list.

      Time Complexity: O(1) Space Complexity: O(1)

      Parameters:
      value - the value to prepend

    • get

      public T get(int index)
      Returns the element at the given index using bidirectional traversal. Traversal starts from the head if index < size / 2, or from the tail otherwise.

      Time Complexity: O(n) worst case; O(n/2) average

      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index of the element to retrieve
      Returns:
      the element at index, or null if out of bounds

    • getFirst

      public T getFirst()
      Returns the first element in the list without removing it.

      Time Complexity: O(1)

      Returns:
      the head element, or null if the list is empty

    • getLast

      public T getLast()
      Returns the last element in the list without removing it.

      Time Complexity: O(1)

      Returns:
      the tail element, or null if the list is empty

    • getWindow

      public List<T> getWindow(int offset, int limit)
      Returns a window (page) of elements from the list. Time Complexity: O(offset + limit) Space Complexity: O(limit)

    • removeFirst

      public T removeFirst()
      Removes and returns the first element in the list.

      Time Complexity: O(1) Space Complexity: O(1)

      Returns:
      the removed head element, or null if the list is empty

    • removeLast

      public T removeLast()
      Removes and returns the last element in the list.

      Time Complexity: O(1) Space Complexity: O(1)

      Returns:
      the removed tail element, or null if the list is empty

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if the list contains no elements.

      Time Complexity: O(1)

      Returns:
      true if the list is empty

    • contains

      public boolean contains(T value)
      Returns true if the list contains an element equal to value according to Object.equals(Object).

      Time Complexity: O(n)

      Parameters:
      value - the value to search for
      Returns:
      true if a matching element is found

    • toList

      public List<T> toList()
      Returns all elements as a new List in head-to-tail order.

      Time Complexity: O(n) Space Complexity: O(n)

      Returns:
      a List containing all elements in order
